summary: Classify repo changes into deterministic AOS developer workflow actions without relying on session memory.

rules.summary: App subtree changes delegate app-specific workflow details to the nearest local contract.

actions.reason: The repo-wide manifest owns platform routing only; app-specific tests, diagnostics, and behavior belong in the nearest subtree AGENTS.md or local playbook.

control_surface.preferred: Read the nearest subtree AGENTS.md and local workflow docs before choosing app-specific tests or diagnostics.
control_surface.ui_projection: Show local app contract delegation, not app internals, from the platform workflow surface.

rules.summary: Swift source changes require a fresh signed repo binary before ./aos-backed verification.

actions.reason: The repo binary must include the changed Swift code, and signing should stay under the AOS build surface.
AOS-backed verification depends on daemon ownership, Accessibility, and Input Monitoring readiness.

verification.when: Run the smallest focused test suite that covers the changed behavior.

human_handoff.instruction: Stop automated repair loops and tell the human the repo-mode aos permission grant is stale; they must remove and re-add aos in Accessibility and Input Monitoring.

control_surface.fallback: bash build.sh only when ./aos cannot run or the build control surface itself is being repaired
control_surface.ui_projection: Show build, ready, and human handoff state as a small developer command tree.

rules.summary: Build-surface changes may need the raw build script once, then must return to the AOS control surface.

actions.reason: Prove the AOS developer control surface owns the build flow; use raw build.sh only if ./aos cannot run.

human_handoff.instruction: Use the normal post-build TCC handoff; do not keep repairing in a loop.

control_surface.fallback: bash build.sh --no-restart only while repairing or introducing ./aos dev build

rules.summary: Toolkit canvas changes should prefer content reload and canvas checks over a Swift rebuild.

actions.reason: Toolkit web assets do not require rebuilding ./aos by default.
Loaded canvases need refreshed content to show web asset changes.

verification.when: Use when a toolkit component is projected through aos:// content.

control_surface.ui_projection: Offer reload and show-wait actions for the affected canvas.

rules.summary: Shared schema changes should run schema fixtures before runtime checks.

actions.reason: Schema and fixture contracts are verified without daemon or display state.

verification.when: Always run after changing shared schema contracts or fixtures.

rules.summary: Docs and recipes usually need review, not rebuild or daemon mutation.

actions.reason: Documentation-only changes should not trigger a binary rebuild unless they document a command that also changed.

control_surface.preferred: review changed docs and run schema tests only for structured docs

rules.summary: Shell tests that invoke ./aos need the binary freshness decision before the test run.

actions.reason: Shell integration tests that invoke ./aos depend on daemon readiness and mode isolation.
Run the focused shell test after readiness is established.

verification.when: Use the specific shell test touched by the change.

human_handoff.instruction: Stop and use the standard post-permission handoff before running the integration test.
